content:
3 min read
Just now

--

it represents eight lives
Sometimes, life overwhelms us with so many struggles that we no longer know what to do or which direction to take. Even so, we keep trying to stand strong. Lately, life has felt incredibly heavy to me.
There was a time when I couldn’t even share my voice. I was a girl who didn’t dare to post a single photo of herself. I didn’t have the courage, and in many ways, I didn’t have the freedom either. Then, one day, I recited a poem in a place where I never expected to. Today, that poem has been viewed more than 71,000 times. For some people, that may seem like a small number. But for me, it means everything. It represents the moment I finally broke my silence.
I didn’t even share my own photo at first. It was shared through an organization, and only then did I find the courage to share it myself. That experience taught me something important: sometimes the hardest step is simply taking the first one.
Life has brought me many other challenges as well. I started a saffron business, but I still don’t have customers. At the same time, I began offering free Turkish language lessons to girls in Afghanistan. Right now, I have only eight students. Maybe eight is a small number to others, but to me, it represents eight lives, eight dreams, and eight opportunities. That alone makes me feel successful.
There are days when I tell myself, “I’m not successful. I can’t do this.” I lose confidence in myself. But then I stand up again and remind myself, “No, I can do this. Giving up is not an option in my life.” Because I’ve realized that success is not about never falling — it’s about getting back up every time you do.
Still, life is not easy. I am 21 years old, and my heart can still be deeply hurt. The most painful moments often come from the people closest to me. Even last week, my heart was broken in ways that left me wondering how I would move forward. Sometimes, I truly don’t know. It hurts more than I can put into words.
The responsibilities I carry are invisible to most people. My worries about the future, the challenges of today, my mother’s expectations, my father’s words, my older brother’s hopes, my younger siblings’ needs — they all weigh on me at the same time. Sometimes it feels as though they are slowly wearing me down. Yet I continue walking forward.
There are moments when all I want is to go somewhere completely quiet, where no one knows me. A place where I can sit in silence — or cry and scream without holding anything back. There is a cry inside me that I have kept buried for a long time. Sometimes it feels like a wound that may never fully heal. Maybe time will heal it. Maybe it will always stay with me. I simply don’t know yet.
But there is one thing I do know: I am still here. I am still fighting. I have not given up.
Perhaps one day, years from now, I will read these words again. Together, we will see whether my pain has changed, whether I have changed. If I have, these words will remind me of where I started. And if I haven’t, they will still remind me that I never stopped trying.
Because sometimes hope is not found in extraordinary victories — it is found in refusing to give up.
